qtype: Speed, distance, time calculations; rates calculation
tex:
Karel travelled from London to Johannesburg and then from Johannesburg to Windhoek.
\newline

(a) The flight from London to Johannesburg took 11 hours 10 minutes.
\newline
The average speed was 813 km/h.
\newline

Calculate the distance travelled from London to Johannesburg.
\newline
Give your answer correct to the nearest 10 km.
\newline

 km

(b) The total time for Karel's journey from London to Windhoek was 15 hours 42 minutes.
\newline
The total distance travelled from London to Windhoek was 10 260 km.
\newline

(i) (c) Calculate the average speed for this journey.
\newline

 km/h

(d) The cost of Karel's journey from London to Windhoek was \$470.
\newline

\begin{subsubparts}

(e) Calculate the distance travelled per dollar.
\newline

 km per dollar

(f) Calculate the cost per 100 km of this journey.
\newline
Give your answer correct to the nearest cent.
\newline

\$  per 100 km

\end{subsubparts}

(g) Karel changed \$300 into 3891 Namibian dollars.
\newline

Complete the statement.
\newline

\$1 =  Namibian dollars

content.content:
The flight from London to Johannesburg took 11 hours 10 minutes.
\newline
The average speed was 813 km/h.
\newline

Calculate the distance travelled from London to Johannesburg.
\newline
Give your answer correct to the nearest 10 km.
\newline

The total time for Karel's journey from London to Windhoek was 15 hours 42 minutes.
\newline
The total distance travelled from London to Windhoek was 10 260 km.
\newline

Calculate the average speed for this journey.
\newline

The cost of Karel's journey from London to Windhoek was \$470.
\newline
Calculate the distance travelled per dollar.

Calculate the cost per 100 km of this journey.
Give your answer correct to the nearest cent.

\$ \AnswerLine[][] per 100 km

Karel changed \$300 into 3891 Namibian dollars.
\newline

Complete the statement.
\newline

\$1 = \AnswerLine[][] Namibian dollars

stem.content:
Karel travelled from London to Johannesburg and then from Johannesburg to Windhoek.
\newline

qtype: Compound interest, percentage increase, exponential decay calculation
tex:
(a) Beth invests \$2000 at a rate of 2\% per year compound interest.
\newline

(i) (b) Calculate the value of this investment at the end of 5 years.
\newline

\$ 

(c) Calculate the overall percentage increase in the value of Beth's investment at the end of 5 years.
\newline

 \%

(d) Calculate the minimum number of complete years it takes for the value of Beth's investment to increase from \$2000 to more than \$2500.
\newline

(e) The population of a village decreases exponentially at a rate of 4\% each year.
\newline
The population is now 255.
\newline

Calculate the population 16 years ago.
\newline

\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

tex:
The height, $h\,\text{cm}$, of each of 120 plants is measured.
\newline
The cumulative frequency diagram shows this information.
\newline

(a) Use the cumulative frequency diagram to find an estimate of

(i)  the median,

 the interquartile range,

 the 60th percentile,

 the number of plants with a height greater than $40\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

(b) The information in the cumulative frequency diagram is shown in this frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Height, $h\,\text{cm}$ & $0 < h \leqslant 10$ & $10 < h \leqslant 20$ & $20 < h \leqslant 30$ & $30 < h \leqslant 50$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 2 & 18 & 62 & 38 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Calculate an estimate of the mean height.
\newline

 A histogram is drawn to show the information in the frequency table.
\newline

The height of the bar representing the interval $10 < h \leqslant 20$ is $7.2\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the height of the bar representing the interval $30 < h \leqslant 50$.
\newline

tex:
Ahmed sells different types of cake in his shop.
\newline
The cost of each cake depends on its type and its size.
\newline

Every small cake costs $\$x$ and every large cake costs $\$(2x + 1)$.
\newline

(a) The total cost of 3 small lemon cakes and 2 large lemon cakes is \$12.36.
\newline

Find the cost of a small lemon cake.
\newline

(b) The cost of 18 small chocolate cakes is the same as the cost of 7 large chocolate cakes.
\newline

Find the cost of a small chocolate cake.
\newline

(c) The number of small cherry cakes that can be bought for \$4 is the same as the number of large cherry cakes that can be bought for \$13.
\newline

Find the cost of a small cherry cake.
\newline

(d) Petra spends \$20 on small coffee cakes and \$10 on large coffee cakes.
\newline
The total number of cakes is 45.
\newline

Write an equation in terms of $x$.
\newline
Solve this equation to find the cost of a small coffee cake.
\newline
Show all your working.
\newline

content.content:
The total cost of 3 small lemon cakes and 2 large lemon cakes is \$12.36.
\newline

Find the cost of a small lemon cake.
\newline

The cost of 18 small chocolate cakes is the same as the cost of 7 large chocolate cakes.
\newline

Find the cost of a small chocolate cake.
\newline

The number of small cherry cakes that can be bought for \$4 is the same as the number of large cherry cakes that can be bought for \$13.
\newline

Find the cost of a small cherry cake.
\newline

Petra spends \$20 on small coffee cakes and \$10 on large coffee cakes.
\newline
The total number of cakes is 45.
\newline

Write an equation in terms of $x$.
\newline
Solve this equation to find the cost of a small coffee cake.
\newline
Show all your working.
\newline

stem.content:
Ahmed sells different types of cake in his shop.
\newline
The cost of each cake depends on its type and its size.
\newline

Every small cake costs $\$x$ and every large cake costs $\$(2x + 1)$.
\newline

_rawBody:
Ahmed sells different types of cake in his shop.
The cost of each cake depends on its type and its size.

Every small cake costs $\$x$ and every large cake costs $\$(2x + 1)$.

\begin{parts}
\item The total cost of 3 small lemon cakes and 2 large lemon cakes is \$12.36\,.

Find the cost of a small lemon cake.
\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item The cost of 18 small chocolate cakes is the same as the cost of 7 large chocolate cakes.

Find the cost of a small chocolate cake.
\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item The number of small cherry cakes that can be bought for \$4 is the same as the number of large cherry cakes that can be bought for \$13.

Find the cost of a small cherry cake.
\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item Petra spends \$20 on small coffee cakes and \$10 on large coffee cakes.
The total number of cakes is 45.

Write an equation in terms of $x$.
Solve this equation to find the cost of a small coffee cake.
Show all your working.
\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{7}
\end{parts}

tex:
The diagram shows six discs.
\newline
Each disc has a colour and a number.
\newline

(a) One disc is picked at random.
\newline

Write down the probability that

(i)  the disc has the number 4,

 the disc is red and has the number 3,

 the disc is blue and has the number 4.
\newline

(b) Two of the six discs are picked at random **without** replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that

(i)  both discs have the number 3,

 both discs have the same colour.
\newline

(c) Two of the six discs are picked at random **with** replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that both discs have the same colour.
\newline

content.content:
One disc is picked at random.
\newline

Write down the probability that
the disc has the number 4,
the disc is red and has the number 3,

the disc is blue and has the number 4.
\newline

Two of the six discs are picked at random \textbf{without} replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that
both discs have the number 3,

both discs have the same colour.
\newline

Two of the six discs are picked at random \textbf{with} replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that both discs have the same colour.
\newline

stem.content:
The diagram shows six discs.
\newline
Each disc has a colour and a number.
\newline

tex:

(a) Complete the table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & 0.2 & 0.3 & 0.5 & 1 & 1.5 & 2 & 2.5 \\
\hline
$y$ & 5.0 & 3.4 & 2.3 & & 2.9 & & 6.7 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}$ for $0.2 \leqslant x \leqslant 2.5$.
\newline

The graph of $y = x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}$ for $-2.5 \leqslant x \leqslant -0.2$ has been drawn for you.
\newline

(c) By drawing suitable straight lines on the grid, solve the following equations.
\newline

(i)  $x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x} = -2$

 $x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x} + x - 1 = 0$

(d) $k$ is an integer and the equation $x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x} = k$ has three solutions.
\newline

Write down a possible value of $k$.
\newline
